---
title: "WP2P4-Descriptive Analysis: summary tables and figures"
author: "Arnaud"
date: "2026-07-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)

library(readr)
library(knitr)
library(here)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
library(dplyr)
```

# Dataset

```{r}
dataset <- read_csv(
  here("output", "dataset_patients_combined.csv.gz"),
  col_types = cols(
    patient_id = col_integer(),
    age = col_double()
  )
)

dataset <- dataset %>%
  select( 
  patient_id,
  index_date,sex,
  imd,ethnicity,
  stp,region,
  has_medication,numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti,
  numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti
)
dataset$index_date <- as.Date(dataset$index_date)
head(dataset)
```

# Table 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume by region
```{r}
table_0_0 <- dataset %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),

    gp_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    pf_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_nitro_volume = gp_nitro_volume + pf_nitro_volume,

    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(region)

kable(
  table_0_0,
  caption = "Table 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume by region."
)
```
#
```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap="Figure 0_0. Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume over time by region."}
table_region_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, region) %>%
  summarise(
    gp_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_gp_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    pf_nitro_volume = sum(numerator_pf_nitrofurantoin_uti, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_nitro_volume = gp_nitro_volume + pf_nitro_volume,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(region != "Missing")

nitro_region_plot <- ggplot(
  table_region_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = total_nitro_volume,
    colour = region,
    group = region
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Nitrofurantoin prescribing volume",
    colour = "Region"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 month",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

nitro_region_plot

# Save as PNG
ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_0_region_nitrofurantoin_volume.png",
  plot = nitro_region_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 10,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```
# Table 0_1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by STP
```{r}
table_stp <- dataset %>%
  group_by(stp) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(stp)

kable(
  table_stp,
  caption = "Table 0_1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by STP."
)
```

# Table 1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by IMD
```{r}
table_imd <- dataset %>%
  group_by(imd) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(imd)

kable(
  table_imd,
  caption = "Table 1. Distribution of patients receiving medication by IMD."
)
```

# Figure 1. Patients receiving medication over time by IMD 

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap="Figure 1. Patients receiving medication over time by IMD."}
table_imd_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, imd) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = sum(has_medication == 0, na.rm = TRUE), # We will use 1 when using real data
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(imd != "Missing")

imd_plot <- ggplot(
  table_imd_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = n_patients,
    colour = imd,
    shape = imd,
    group = imd
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of patients",
    colour = "IMD",
    shape = "IMD"
    
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 months",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
  imd_plot 
 # Save as PNG 
ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_imd_plot.png",
  plot = imd_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```

# Table 2. Distribution of patients receiving medication by Region

```{r}
table_region <- dataset %>%
  group_by(region) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(region)

kable(
  table_region,
  caption = "Table 2. Distribution of patients receiving medication by Region."
)
```

# Figure 2. Patients receiving medication over time by Region

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap="Figure 2. Patients receiving medication over time by Region."}
table_region_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, region) %>%
  summarise(
     patients = sum(has_medication == 0, na.rm = TRUE), #n_patients = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(region != "Missing")

region_plot <- ggplot(
  table_region_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = patients,
    colour = region,
    group = region
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of patients",
    colour = "Region"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 months",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_region_plot.png",
  plot = region_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```
# Table 3. Distribution of patients receiving medication by Sex

```{r}
table_sex <- dataset %>%
  group_by(sex) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(sex)

kable(
  table_sex,
  caption = "Table 3. Distribution of patients receiving medication by sex."
)
```

# Figure 3. Patients receiving medication over time by Sex

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap="Figure 3. Patients receiving medication over time by sex."}

table_sex_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, sex) %>%
  summarise(
    patients = sum(has_medication == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sex))

sex_plot <- ggplot(
  table_sex_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = patients,
    colour = sex,
    group = sex
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of patients",
    colour = "Sex"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 months",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

sex_plot

ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_sex_plot.png",
  plot = sex_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```

# Table 4. Distribution of patients receiving medication by Ethnicity

```{r}
table_ethnicity <- dataset %>%
  group_by(ethnicity) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = n(),
    n_with_medication = sum(has_medication == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    percent_with_medication =
      round(100 * n_with_medication / n_patients, 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(ethnicity)

kable(
  table_ethnicity,
  caption = "Table 4. Distribution of patients receiving medication by ethnicity."
)
```

# Figure 4. Patients receiving medication over time by Ethnicity

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=5, fig.cap="Figure 4. Patients receiving medication over time by ethnicity."}
table_ethnicity_plot <- dataset %>%
  group_by(index_date, ethnicity) %>%
  summarise(
    n_patients = sum(has_medication == 0, na.rm = TRUE), #n_patients = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(ethnicity),
    ethnicity != "Missing"
  )

ethnicity_plot <- ggplot(
  table_ethnicity_plot,
  aes(
    x = index_date,
    y = n_patients,
    colour = ethnicity,
    group = ethnicity
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "Number of patients",
    colour = "Ethnicity"
  ) +
  scale_x_date(
    date_breaks = "1 months",
    date_labels = "%Y-%m"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
ggsave(
  filename = "results_0_ethnicity_plot.png",
  plot = ethnicity_plot,
  path = here::here("results_Arnaud"),
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 300
)
```

# Model 
## Interrupted Time Series (ITS) Analysis Using GEE

```{r gee-model, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Load required packages
library(readr)
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(geepack)

#--------------------------------------------------
# Load measures dataset
#--------------------------------------------------

measures <- read_csv(
  here("output", "data_patients_measures_Arnaud.csv")
)

# Inspect data
names(measures)
table(measures$measure)

# Keep GP medication outcome only
measures <- measures %>%
  filter(measure == "gp_medication_all_conditions")

head(measures)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------
# ITS variables : covariates for time, intervention, and interaction
#--------------------------------------------------------------------

measures <- measures %>%
  mutate(
    month_date = as.Date(interval_start)
  ) %>%
  group_by(practice) %>%
  arrange(month_date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    T = row_number() - 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    X = if_else(month_date >= as.Date("2024-02-01"), 1, 0),
    XT = if_else(
      X == 1,
      T - min(T[X == 1]),
      0
    )
  )

head(measures)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fit (initial GEE model)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------
gee_model_1 <- geeglm(
  numerator ~ T + X + XT +
    offset(log(denominator)),
  id = practice,
  data = measures,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  corstr = "ar1"
)

summary(gee_model_1)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------
# Simulate data (for illustration only)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------

set.seed(12345)

measures <- measures %>%
  mutate(

    denominator = round(rnorm(n(), mean = 120, sd = 20)),
    denominator = pmax(denominator, 30),

    prescribing_prob =
      0.65 -
      0.0015 * T -
      0.05 * X -
      0.001 * XT,

    prescribing_prob =
      pmin(
        pmax(prescribing_prob, 0.10),
        0.95
      ),

    numerator = rbinom(
      n(),
      size = denominator,
      prob = prescribing_prob
    ),

    prescribing_rate =
      numerator / denominator
  )

#--------------------------------------------------------------------
# Refit model
#--------------------------------------------------------------------

gee_model_2 <- geeglm(
  numerator ~ T + X + XT +
    offset(log(denominator)),
  id = practice,
  data = measures,
  family = poisson(link = "log"),
  corstr = "ar1"
)

summary(gee_model_2)

#--------------------------------------------------------------------
# Predictions
#--------------------------------------------------------------------

measures$pred <- predict(
  gee_model_2,
  type = "response"
)
#--------------------------------------------------------------------
# Aggregate for plotting
#--------------------------------------------------------------------
plot_data <- measures %>%
  group_by(month_date) %>%
  summarise(
    observed = mean(numerator),
    predicted = mean(pred),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot observed -predicted 
#---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot(plot_data, aes(month_date)) +

  geom_line(
    aes(y = observed,
        colour = "Observed"),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  geom_line(
    aes(y = predicted,
        colour = "Predicted"),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2024-02-01"),
    linetype = "dashed",
    colour = "red"
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      Observed = "black",
      Predicted = "blue"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "GP medication count",
    colour = "",
    title = "Interrupted Time Series using GEE",
    subtitle = "Observed and model-predicted GP prescribing"
  ) +

  theme_bw()

#--------------------------------------------------
# Counterfactual predictions
#--------------------------------------------------

measures$fitted <- predict(
  gee_model_2,
  type = "response"
)

cf_data <- measures

cf_data$X <- 0
cf_data$XT <- 0

cf_data$counterfactual <- predict(
  gee_model_2,
  newdata = cf_data,
  type = "response"
)

measures$counterfactual <- cf_data$counterfactual

#--------------------------------------------------
# Aggregate for counterfactual plot
#--------------------------------------------------

plot_data <- measures %>%
  group_by(month_date) %>%
  summarise(
    observed = mean(numerator),
    fitted = mean(fitted),
    counterfactual = mean(counterfactual),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#--------------------------------------------------
# Plot counterfactual
#--------------------------------------------------

ggplot(plot_data, aes(month_date)) +

  geom_line(
    aes(
      y = observed,
      colour = "Observed"
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +

  geom_line(
    aes(
      y = counterfactual,
      colour = "Counterfactual"
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +

  geom_vline(
    xintercept = as.Date("2024-02-01"),
    linetype = "dotted"
  ) +

  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(
      Observed = "black",
      Counterfactual = "blue"
    )
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "Month",
    y = "GP medication count",
    colour = "",
    title = "Interrupted Time Series Using GEE",
    subtitle = "Counterfactual assumes Pharmacy First had not been introduced"
  ) +

  theme_classic()

```
